area: MSC 82B05, 82B20, 05A15 (with 11B37/37A45 contact) — Statistical mechanics of Collatz parity words: transfer matrices, partition-function (Lee-Yang) zeros, large deviations beyond drift
hole_justification: Searches run: arXiv API metadata queries all:"Collatz" AND all:"Lee-Yang"/"Yang-Lee"/"partition function zeros" (0 results), all:"Collatz" AND all:"large deviations" (0 results), all:"Collatz" AND all:"exponential sums" (0 results); web searches "Lee-Yang Collatz zeros", "Collatz partition function statistical mechanics parity", "Collatz spin chain / Ising / Fisher zeros arXiv", "Collatz large deviations divergent trajectory rate function", "3x+1 transfer matrix/operator parity words", "Collatz thermodynamic formalism / topological pressure", "mathoverflow Collatz statistical mechanics/Lee-Yang", plus targeted checks on Lagarias-Weiss, Kontorovich-Sinai, Berg-Meinardus, Matthews-Watts, Halbeisen-Hungerbühler, Eliahou, Hohertz, Tao. Verdict is split: the LARGE-DEVIATION side is genuinely explored — Lagarias-Weiss (Ann. Appl. Prob. 1992) explicitly applies LD theory to two stochastic models of T (random walk imitating T mod 2^j; branching random walks imitating T^{-1} mod 3^j), computes rate functions, and predicts the extremal stopping-time constant ~41.677 and max-excursion exponent 2; Kontorovich-Sinai 2002/Sinai 2003 give the structure theorem and Gaussian fluctuations; Krasikov-Lagarias 2003 is a rigorous nonnegative transfer-system (Perron eigenvalue/LP) bound x^0.84 on actual orbits; Matthews-Watts 1984-85 built Markov transition matrices on residue classes for generalized maps. The LEE-YANG / ZERO-LOCUS side is essentially virgin: the only "zeros + Collatz" paper is Hohertz arXiv:2012.08006, which studies polynomials whose coefficients are orbit VALUES (Eneström-Kakeya bounds, no dynamical consequence — a different object entirely); Bell-Lagarias arXiv:1408.6884 (natural boundaries of inverse-orbit generating functions) is analyticity-adjacent but not zero-locus of weight ensembles; Santana arXiv:2601.03297 gives conditional thermodynamic-formalism statements (equilibrium states vs. cycle periods) with no partition-function zeros and no computations. Nobody has defined the cycle-counting partition function over parity words, studied its complex zeros, or attempted Fourier/character-sum equidistribution of Böhm-Sontacchi weights at the cycle modulus 2^K−3^L; the unique adjacent rigorous estimate is Tao 2019's superpolynomial Fourier decay of Syracuse random variables at 3-power moduli only.
bridge: Objects: length-K parity words v ∈ {0,1}^K. Terras (1976): n ↦ (parities of T^0(n)..T^{K-1}(n)) is a bijection Z/2^K → {0,1}^K, so the unconstrained parity ensemble is exactly Bernoulli(1/2)^K, and the naive partition function Z_K(z) = Σ_v z^{#odd(v)} = (1+z)^K is trivial — all Lee-Yang zeros sit at z = −1, free energy log(1+z) is analytic at the physical point, and Cramér's theorem gives the exact rate function I(p) = log 2 − H(p) for ones-density p, with divergence requiring prefix densities p ≥ log2/log3 ≈ 0.6309 (this level is mined: it reproduces Terras/Everett and Lagarias-Weiss). The real bridge is the CONSTRAINED ensemble: cycles of signature (K,L) correspond (Böhm-Sontacchi) to words v with odd positions 0 = b_0 < b_1 < ... < b_{L−1} < K satisfying M | W(v), where W(v) = Σ_{i=0}^{L−1} 3^{L−1−i} 2^{b_i} and M = 2^K − 3^L; hence #cycles(K,L) = (1/M) Σ_{ξ mod M} S_{K,L}(ξ) with S_{K,L}(ξ) = Σ_v e^{2πi ξ W(v)/M}. Dictionary: parity word ↔ spin configuration; #odd ↔ magnetization; W(v) ↔ energy; e^{2πiξ/M} ↔ fugacity ON the unit circle (the literal Lee-Yang locus); S_{K,L}(ξ) ↔ partition function with complex Boltzmann weights, computable as an inhomogeneous transfer-matrix product over positions j = 0..K−1 with state = number of odd steps used so far; cycle existence ↔ failure of cancellation of Z at roots of unity; divergent orbits ↔ configurations of the "ballistic" subshift {all prefix ones-densities eventually ≥ log2/log3}, a conditioned ensemble whose pressure and Beraha-Kahane-Weiss zero-accumulation curves govern what any finite-state (DFA/transfer-matrix) divergence certificate can express.
strongest_adjacent_theorems: Lee-Yang circle theorem (Lee & Yang, Phys. Rev. 87, 1952) with Asano/Ruelle contraction extensions, Lieb-Sokal 1981, and the Borcea-Brändén classification of zero-locus-preserving operators (Invent. Math. 177, 2009): fugacity zeros of ferromagnetic partition functions confined to |z| = 1 — exactly the locus where the character weights e^{2πiξW(v)/M} live.

Beraha-Kahane-Weiss theorem (J. Combin. Theory Ser. B 28, 1980 / earlier 1975 announcement): limit points of zeros of f_n(z) = Σ_i α_i(z) λ_i(z)^n are precisely the equimodularity curves of dominant transfer-matrix eigenvalues (developed at scale by Salas-Sokal for Potts/chromatic zeros) — the exact tool for zero loci of regular-language-constrained parity ensembles.

Odlyzko & Poonen, 'Zeros of polynomials with 0,1 coefficients' (Enseign. Math. 39, 1993, 317-348): all zeros of {0,1}-polynomials lie in an explicit annulus (nontrivial zeros between the golden-ratio bounds), and the closure of the zero set is path-connected — the model theorem for restricted-coefficient families like the cycle numerators P_v(x) = Σ 3^{L−1−i} x^{b_i} with P_v(2) = W(v).

Lagarias & Weiss, 'The 3x+1 problem: two stochastic models' (Ann. Appl. Prob. 2, 1992, 229-261): exact large-deviation rate functions for random-walk and branching-random-walk models of T; predicted extremal constant γ_BP ≈ 41.677647 for max total stopping time and excursion exponent 2, matching empirical records.

Krasikov & Lagarias, 'Bounds for the 3x+1 problem using difference inequalities' (Acta Arith. 109, 2003; arXiv:math/0205002): #{n ≤ x eventually reaching 1} ≥ x^0.84, proved by a nonnegative system of difference inequalities mod 3^k — a rigorous Perron-eigenvalue/transfer-system bound on actual orbits, showing the transfer-matrix mechanism can produce unconditional Collatz theorems.

Tao (arXiv:1909.03562, Forum of Math. Pi 2022), Fourier decay of Syracuse random variables: |E e(−ξ Syrac(Z/3^n)/3^n)| ≪_A n^{−A} for 3 ∤ ξ — superpolynomial decay of the characteristic function of exactly a Böhm-Sontacchi-type random weight, but at 3-power moduli, not at the cycle modulus 2^K − 3^L.
the_push: Two pushes, one per open half. (A) CYCLES: since the number of admissible words C(K−1, L−1) ≈ 2^{K·H(L/K)} with H(log2/log3) ≈ 0.95 bits, while M = 2^K − 3^L ≈ 2^K at admissible signatures, the ratio C/M decays exponentially; therefore ANY uniform power saving |S_{K,L}(ξ)| ≤ C(K−1,L−1)^{1−δ} for all ξ ≠ 0 would prove finiteness of Collatz cycles outright — an attack orthogonal to Baker's method: linear forms in logarithms bound |2^K − 3^L| from below, whereas this bounds the COUNT of residue-condition solutions via equidistribution in (Z/M)^*, where the built-in relation 2^K ≡ 3^L (mod M) makes the subgroup ⟨2,3⟩ nearly cyclic — structure nobody has exploited. Honest caveat: uniform decay for all ξ likely embeds ×2×3-equidistribution difficulties (Furstenberg-rigidity / Erdős ternary-digits adjacent), so the realistic deliverable is decay on structured/major-arc ξ plus ⟨2⟩-orbit symmetry, yielding exclusions of specific signature families and a clean reduction theorem ('square-root cancellation at cycle moduli ⇒ finitely many cycles') that currently exists nowhere in the literature. (B) DIVERGENCE: divergent parity words live in the ballistic subshift (prefix ones-density ≥ log2/log3 eventually — the known first-order constraint); any regular divergence certificate induces a tilted transfer matrix whose Perron pressure must simultaneously satisfy entropy, drift, and Terras-realizability (Bernoulli-1/2 compatibility) inequalities, and Beraha-Kahane-Weiss equimodularity analysis of these matrices can convert the program's exhaustive 'no ≤7-state DFA certificate' result into an asymptotic lower bound on certificate size, plus genuinely new second-order (fluctuation-level) constraints on the parity statistics a divergent orbit must maintain forever — beyond the drift bound, which is all that is currently known.
first_move: Implement the inhomogeneous transfer-matrix/DP evaluation of S_{K,L}(ξ) = Σ_{v:(K,L)} e^{2πiξW(v)/M} (DP over positions j = 0..K−1, state = number of odd steps used; O(K·L) complex operations per ξ; M = 2^K − 3^L in exact bignum, phases in double precision) for all admissible signatures with K ≤ 5000 near the continued-fraction convergents of log2/log3, and map |S(ξ)|/C(K−1,L−1) across ξ on the 32-core machines. Simultaneously attempt the first lemma, which the computation will confirm or kill: (i) the symmetry lemma — cyclic rotation of parity words induces S_{K,L}(2ξ mod M) = (unit phase)·S_{K,L}(ξ), so |S| is constant on ⟨2⟩-orbits in Z/M, collapsing the frequency space by ord_M(2) and exposing the ⟨2,3⟩-subgroup structure (2^K ≡ 3^L mod M); this is elementary and Lean-formalizable as a warm-up; then (ii) the decay lemma — transplant Tao's Syracuse characteristic-function estimate (skew random walk mechanism, his Section 5-6 of arXiv:1909.03562) from modulus 3^n to modulus M: bound |S(ξ)| ≤ C(K−1,L−1)·ρ^{t(ξ)} where t(ξ) counts DP steps at which the phase increment ξ·3^{L−1−i}2^j/M is quantitatively far from 0 mod 1. If empirical square-root cancellation holds uniformly (expected), write the reduction theorem 'uniform power-saving on S at admissible signatures ⇒ finitely many Collatz cycles' as the first paper.
payoff_odds: Best case (probability under 2-3%): a proven uniform power-saving bound on S_{K,L}(ξ) at all admissible signatures, giving finiteness of Collatz cycles — this would exceed Hercher 2023 and all Baker-method results by an order of magnitude and settle half the negative side of the conjecture; the low probability reflects that full uniformity likely inherits ×2×3-rigidity hardness. Likely case (30-50%): (i) the ⟨2⟩-orbit symmetry lemma plus major-arc decay estimates, an empirical zero/cancellation atlas at convergent signatures, and a publishable, genuinely novel reduction theorem (no Collatz + exponential-sums/Lee-Yang paper exists — arXiv metadata searches return zero); possibly new unconditional exclusions of specific (K,L) families complementary to Baker; (ii) a pressure/Beraha-Kahane-Weiss lower-bound theorem on the size of any DFA divergence certificate, directly extending the program's exhaustive ≤7-state result into an asymptotic statement. Failure mode to state plainly: literal Lee-Yang positivity technology (circle theorems, Asano contractions) probably does NOT apply because the weights are characters rather than ferromagnetic couplings — the operative tools are BKW equimodularity and exponential sums; and the pure large-deviations-beyond-drift line, pursued alone, mostly reproduces Terras/Everett/Lagarias-Weiss/Krasikov-Lagarias and is already mined. Expect solid partial-progress papers and program-relevant certificate bounds, not a resolution.

prior_art.ref: Lagarias & Weiss, 'The 3x+1 problem: two stochastic models', Ann. Appl. Prob. 2(1), 1992, 229-261
prior_art.what: THE large-deviation prior art: explicit LD rate functions for a random walk imitating T mod 2^j and branching random walks imitating T^{-1} mod 3^j; predicts extremal stopping-time constant ~41.677 and excursion exponent 2. Models only — no constraints derived on actual orbits, no complex zeros.

prior_art.ref: Kontorovich & Sinai, 'Structure theorem for (d,g,h)-maps', Bull. Braz. Math. Soc. 33(2), 2002, 213-224; Sinai, 'Statistical (3x+1) problem', CPAM 56, 2003; Kontorovich & Lagarias, 'Stochastic models for the 3x+1 and 5x+1 problems', 2009
prior_art.what: CLT/Brownian fluctuation structure for log-iterates; survey of stochastic predictions. Again model-level; no partition-function zeros, no cycle-modulus Fourier analysis.

prior_art.ref: Matthews & Watts, 'A Markov approach to the generalized Syracuse algorithm', Acta Arith. 45, 1985 (and 1984 companion); Matthews, 'Generalized 3x+1 mappings: Markov chains and ergodic theory' (survey in The Ultimate Challenge, AMS 2010)
prior_art.what: Transfer/transition matrices on residue classes mod m for generalized Collatz maps; conjectural limiting frequencies for divergent trajectories from ergodic states of finite Markov chains. Real transfer-matrix prior art, but no complex spectral/zero analysis and nothing at cycle moduli.

prior_art.ref: Krasikov & Lagarias, 'Bounds for the 3x+1 problem using difference inequalities', Acta Arith. 109, 2003 (arXiv:math/0205002); Applegate & Lagarias, 'Density bounds for the 3x+1 problem I-II', Math. Comp. 64, 1995
prior_art.what: Rigorous nonnegative transfer-system (Perron/LP) bounds on actual orbits: density of n reaching 1 is at least x^0.84. The rigorous success story of the transfer-matrix mechanism — real-eigenvalue only, no zero loci.

prior_art.ref: Tao, 'Almost all orbits of the Collatz map attain almost bounded values', arXiv:1909.03562, Forum of Math. Pi 10 (2022)
prior_art.what: Proves superpolynomial Fourier decay of the characteristic function of Syracuse random variables at moduli 3^n via a skew random walk on a 3-adic cyclic group — the unique rigorous equidistribution estimate for Böhm-Sontacchi-type weights; done only at 3-power moduli, never at the cycle modulus 2^K−3^L.

prior_art.ref: Hohertz, 'Bounds for zeros of Collatz polynomials, with necessary and sufficient strictness conditions', arXiv:2012.08006
prior_art.what: The only existing 'zeros + Collatz' paper: polynomials whose COEFFICIENTS are orbit values, bounded via Eneström-Kakeya; zeros on |z|=2 shown sparse. A different object with no dynamical consequences — confirms the Lee-Yang/cycle-polynomial hole is unmined.

prior_art.ref: Bell & Lagarias, '3x+1 inverse orbit generating functions almost always have natural boundaries', arXiv:1408.6884
prior_art.what: Analyticity/singularity-locus result for inverse-orbit generating functions — the spiritually closest complex-analytic work; boundaries, not zero distributions of weighted ensembles.

prior_art.ref: Berg & Meinardus, 'Functional equations connected with the Collatz problem', Results Math. 25 (1994) / Rostock. Math. Kolloq. 48 (1995)
prior_art.what: Collatz conjecture reformulated as uniqueness of solutions of a functional equation on the unit disk = fixed points of a linear (transfer-type) operator on holomorphic functions; no spectral zeros/pressure computed.

prior_art.ref: Santana, 'On the Collatz conjecture: topological and ergodic approach', arXiv:2601.03297 (2026)
prior_art.what: Conditional thermodynamic-formalism statements: existence/uniqueness of equilibrium states for continuous potentials tied to finiteness/periods of cycles and to divergent orbits. No partition-function zeros, no transfer-matrix computations, no arithmetic input — the lone (fringe) thermodynamic-formalism paper.

prior_art.ref: Halbeisen & Hungerbühler, 'Optimal bounds for the length of rational Collatz cycles', Acta Arith. 78, 1997; Eliahou, 'The 3x+1 problem: new lower bounds on nontrivial cycle lengths', Discrete Math. 118, 1993; Hercher 2023
prior_art.what: Extremal optimization over parity vectors (entropy-flavored) + continued-fraction convergents of log_2 3 forcing cycle signatures; the benchmarks any zero-locus/character-sum cycle exclusion must beat or complement.

prior_art.ref: Odlyzko & Poonen, 'Zeros of polynomials with 0,1 coefficients', Enseign. Math. 39, 1993, 317-348
prior_art.what: Bridge material from the other side: explicit annulus confinement and path-connectedness of the zero set for restricted-coefficient polynomial families — the template for analyzing the cycle numerators P_v(x) = Σ 3^{L−1−i} x^{b_i}.
